title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your college principal has asked you to write a report on how students spend their free time and what leisure facilities they would like to see improved. Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Leisure Activities and Facility Improvements

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to outline how students at our college currently spend their free time and to recommend improvements to the existing leisure facilities. The findings are based on a survey of 200 students conducted last month.

Current Leisure Activities
The survey revealed that the majority of students spend their free time engaging in physical activities such as football and swimming, while a significant proportion prefer socialising in the campus café. A smaller but notable group expressed interest in creative pursuits, including music and art.

Existing Facilities
While the college gymnasium is generally well-equipped, students reported that it is frequently overcrowded during peak hours. The outdoor sports pitches are in need of resurfacing, and the common room lacks adequate seating and recreational equipment. Several respondents highlighted the absence of a dedicated space for creative activities.

Recommendations
Based on the findings, I would recommend the following measures:
- Extending gymnasium opening hours to reduce overcrowding
- Resurfacing the outdoor pitches to ensure they are safe and usable year-round
- Creating a multipurpose creative studio where students can pursue artistic interests
- Upgrading the common room with comfortable seating and games facilities

Conclusion
Implementing these recommendations would significantly enhance students' well-being and foster a more vibrant campus community. I strongly urge the college to prioritise these improvements in the forthcoming budget.

tasks.title: Email/Letter
tasks.prompt: You have received an email from an English-speaking friend who is planning to visit your country for the first time. Write an email giving advice about what to see and do, and what to pack.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Sarah,

I was absolutely thrilled to hear that you're planning to visit Spain next month! I'm sure you'll have a wonderful time, and I'd love to share some suggestions to help you make the most of your trip.

First of all, I'd strongly recommend spending a few days in Barcelona, where you can marvel at Gaudí's extraordinary architecture, particularly the Sagrada Familia and Park Güell. If you're a food enthusiast, you simply must visit La Boqueria market — the variety of fresh produce and local delicacies is truly remarkable.

From there, I'd suggest heading south to Andalucía. Seville and Granada are absolute must-sees; the Alhambra palace is breathtaking, and the flamenco shows in Seville are an unforgettable cultural experience. Bear in mind that March can still be rather unpredictable weather-wise, so I'd advise packing layers — light clothing for the warmer days alongside a waterproof jacket and a warm jumper for cooler evenings.

Comfortable walking shoes are essential, as many of the most beautiful areas are best explored on foot. You might also want to bring a good sunscreen, as the Spanish sun can be surprisingly strong even in spring.

Do let me know your exact dates so we can arrange to meet up. I'd be more than happy to show you around my city!

Looking forward to seeing you soon,
Carlos

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You have seen this announcement in an international magazine:
'We are looking for reviews of books or films that made you think differently about the world. Tell us what it was, why you recommend it, and what impact it had on you.'
Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
A Film That Changed My Perspective: 'The Pursuit of Happyness'

Based on the remarkable true story of Chris Gardner, 'The Pursuit of Happyness' is a profoundly moving film that challenges our assumptions about success, resilience, and the human spirit. Directed by Gabriele Muccino and starring Will Smith in what is arguably his finest performance, this 2006 drama left an indelible impression on me.

The film follows Gardner, a struggling salesman who finds himself homeless with his young son after a series of devastating setbacks. What makes this story so compelling is not merely the hardship he endures, but the extraordinary determination with which he confronts each obstacle. The scenes depicting their nights in homeless shelters and public bathrooms are handled with remarkable sensitivity, never resorting to sentimentality.

What struck me most profoundly was how the film redefines success. Rather than portraying it as the accumulation of wealth, it presents success as the courage to persevere when everything seems hopeless. Gardner's unwavering commitment to his son serves as a powerful reminder that true strength lies in our relationships and our refusal to surrender our dreams.

Since watching this film, I have found myself approaching challenges with considerably greater resilience. It taught me that setbacks are not permanent and that determination can ultimately triumph over adversity.

I would wholeheartedly recommend this film to anyone seeking inspiration. It is a testament to the extraordinary capacity of the human spirit to overcome seemingly insurmountable odds.
